expectations.text: Creates a new input assumption with Inp_ prefix for the depreciation rate (e.g., Inp_Depreciation_Pct = 0.10)
expectations.evidence: No input assumption with Inp_ prefix was created. The depreciation rate is hardcoded directly in formulas: 'B7 = =Revenue_Y1*0.1'. The value 0.1 is embedded as a magic number, not as a Named Range input.

expectations.text: Creates calculation Named Ranges for Depreciation_Y1, Depreciation_Y2, Depreciation_Y3
expectations.evidence: Named Ranges section shows: Depreciation_Y1 -> Calculations!$B$7, Depreciation_Y2 -> Calculations!$C$7, Depreciation_Y3 -> Calculations!$D$7.

expectations.text: Depreciation formulas use Named Ranges only (e.g., Depreciation_Y1 = Revenue_Y1 * Inp_Depreciation_Pct)
expectations.evidence: Formulas use hardcoded 0.1: '=Revenue_Y1*0.1', '=Revenue_Y2*0.1', '=Revenue_Y3*0.1'. The 0.1 is a magic number, not a Named Range reference.

expectations.text: Updates existing EBITDA calculations to subtract depreciation (or creates new EBIT Named Ranges)
expectations.evidence: Report states EBITDA formulas updated: 'After: =Gross_Profit_Y1-OpEx_Y1-Depreciation_Y1'. Note: this modifies EBITDA rather than creating EBIT, which the assertion allows as an option.

expectations.text: Uses idfa-ops write/create-range commands for all model modifications
expectations.evidence: No mention of idfa-ops scripts. The report uses openpyxl directly to modify the model.

expectations.text: Verifies the updated model produces correct values via recalculate and read
expectations.evidence: No recalculation or read verification. Expected values are listed but computed internally, not verified via model recalculation.
